$comment: Machine-readable seed manifest for the synthetic patient corpus (PRD-009). Maps each seeded scenario S1-S6 to the SourceDocument ids in app/src/data/corpus.ts that carry it, so PRD-008's demo script lands on cue and PRD-010's detection can be scored against known truth. The shipped demo corpus is the deterministic one in app/src/data/corpus.ts; this manifest names what it contains.

seeds.name: Corrected laboratory result supersedes the earlier one
seeds.description: A raised potassium is later corrected to normal on an amended report; recency and validity govern the supersession, not text similarity.
seeds.shows: Supersession: the newer corrected value wins; the superseded value is retained, not deleted.

seeds.name: Discharge medication contradicts the GP repeat list
seeds.description: The June discharge summary records amlodipine 10mg while the September GP repeat list still shows 5mg; the two are surfaced as a contradiction, never silently reconciled.
seeds.shows: Contradiction surfacing — the flagship demo moment (PRD-008 act 5).

seeds.name: Duplicated clinic letter
seeds.description: A near-identical copy of the cardiology clinic letter; de-duplicated without losing either document's provenance.
seeds.shows: De-duplication that keeps both documents' provenance intact.

seeds.name: Referral to clinic to discharge cross-reference chain
seeds.description: The dose is set at referral (amlodipine 5mg), raised at the cardiology clinic (10mg), and carried at discharge (10mg); the chain is followed across three documents with attribution.
seeds.shows: Cross-document reference following and attribution along a referral-to-discharge chain.

seeds.name: Working diagnosis later refuted
seeds.description: Stable angina is recorded at discharge as a working diagnosis, then not supported by the later CT coronary angiography; the Claim is refuted and retained, not removed.
seeds.shows: A Claim's mutability: refuted and retained, not deleted.

seeds.name: Allergy present in some documents, absent from others
seeds.description: A penicillin allergy is recorded in the clinic letter and the discharge summary, but absent from the earlier referral (which records no known drug allergies) and from the GP repeat list; absence is a finding, and the allergy is weighted to persist.
seeds.shows: Absence as a finding; staleness weighting (allergies persist).
